title: Polish save tooltip and friendly Open With build
description: Add a Ctrl+S save tooltip, remove the perishable screenshot, and provide a no-registry friendly-named Windows executable copy for Open With when Windows displays the raw executable basename.
acceptance_criteria: Save control tooltip mentions Ctrl+S; screenshot is removed; a repeatable build/package command creates Markdown Reader.exe without registry writes; validation passes.
notes:
Implementation started. Screenshot was an untracked perishable reference and has been removed. Open With still showing the raw exe name appears to be Windows using the executable basename for an unregistered app; adding a friendly-named build copy avoids registry writes.
Added Save (Ctrl+S) tooltip and test coverage. Removed the untracked perishable screenshot from assets. Added scripts/build-windows.cmd because the initial PowerShell script was blocked by local execution policy; the cmd wrapper runs cargo build/cargo build --release and copies markdown-reader.exe to a friendly named Markdown Reader.exe next to it. Verified target\\release\\Markdown Reader.exe exists, has subsystem 2, FileDescription/ProductName Markdown Reader, and an extractable 32x32 icon. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo build, cargo build --release, cmd /c scripts\\build-windows.cmd --release, metadata/icon checks, and git diff --check.
Committed friendly build copy and save tooltip in 8905daa1d59457b431f73a7beb5e381762765c01 (Add friendly Windows build copy).
close_reason: Save tooltip, screenshot cleanup, and no-registry friendly Open With executable copy are implemented and validated.

title: Polish Windows app identity and launch shortcuts
description: Polish Windows app identity and interaction: show user-friendly Markdown Reader name in Open With metadata without registry changes, remove the console window for launched builds, and add Alt+Left/Alt+Right keyboard shortcuts with toggle tooltip text.
acceptance_criteria: Windows resource metadata uses Markdown Reader display strings; app executable builds as Windows GUI subsystem without console; Alt+Left switches to plaintext, Alt+Right switches to formatted view; slider tooltip mentions hotkeys; validation passes.
notes:
Implementation started. Scope: VERSIONINFO display strings, no-console subsystem behavior, Alt+Left/Alt+Right view toggle shortcuts, and tooltip polish. User-provided screenshot in assets is left untracked unless explicitly requested.
Implemented Windows metadata and hotkey polish. build.rs now sets FileDescription/ProductName/InternalName to Markdown Reader and OriginalFilename to markdown-reader.exe. Windows subsystem attribute is now applied for all Windows builds, including debug, so target\\debug\\markdown-reader.exe and target\\release\\markdown-reader.exe both verify as subsystem 2 (Windows GUI). Added Alt+Left for plaintext view, Alt+Right for formatted view, and toggle tooltip text with the shortcuts. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo build, cargo build --release, PE metadata/subsystem checks, and associated icon extraction. User screenshot in assets was left untracked.
Committed polish changes in b9130bf6b5f7d57f4d874aa21be6e53f2305e6b5 (Polish Windows identity and view shortcuts).
close_reason: Windows display metadata, no-console subsystem behavior, and Alt+Arrow view shortcuts are implemented and validated.

title: Flush rendered scrollbar to window edge
description: Formatted Markdown view leaves the scroll container inset from the right edge, making the rendered view feel narrower than the window. Adjust layout so the rendered scroll area reaches the right edge while preserving readable document content padding.
acceptance_criteria: Formatted view scrollbar sits at the right edge of the content window; rendered content remains readable; release build verifies no console window; validation passes.
notes:
Implementation started. Need rendered view scroll container to fill the available pane width and release GUI verification.
Removed built-in theme max-width/margin rules from #rendered so the formatted view scroll container remains full width and the scrollbar sits at the pane edge. Readability still comes from the base #rendered padding. Release build verified as Windows GUI subsystem; startup smoke stayed alive 5 seconds. Plain cargo build could not replace target\\debug\\markdown-reader.exe because an existing debug instance is still running and locking that file.
Committed rendered layout cleanup in 655ab060ff63683eadac3187a9fdf3ec90b91e64 (Fix runtime icon and rendered layout).
Correction: exact commit hash is 655ab063aec8386d167e0626e43c9c2737106beb.
close_reason: Rendered scroll container no longer gets inset by built-in themes; release GUI validation passed.

title: Show icon in running app window
description: The executable resource icon is embedded, but the running window does not show the app icon in the top-left title bar. Add a runtime window icon so the active app window uses the generated icon.
acceptance_criteria: Running app window has a runtime icon configured; release build still embeds icon; validation passes.
notes:
Implementation started. Need runtime tao window icon in addition to Windows executable resource icon.
Added runtime window icon via tao WindowBuilder::with_window_icon using generated assets/app-icon-32.rgba derived from the app icon PNG. Release exe still embeds assets/app-icon.ico. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo build --release, PE subsystem check, associated icon extraction, and release startup smoke.
Committed runtime icon fix in 655ab060ff63683eadac3187a9fdf3ec90b91e64 (Fix runtime icon and rendered layout).
Correction: exact commit hash is 655ab063aec8386d167e0626e43c9c2737106beb.
close_reason: Runtime window icon is configured from the generated icon asset and validation passed.

title: Security review Markdown Reader local app
description: Review the app for corporate-security-sensitive flags such as registry writes, remote execution, script execution from Markdown, unsafe persistence, surprising file writes, and external network loading. Clean up practical issues and document residual risks.
acceptance_criteria: Review findings are recorded; app avoids registry writes; Markdown scripts remain sanitized/non-executable; close does not autosave; external navigation is blocked; residual risks are documented; validation passes.
notes:
Security review started. Focus areas: AppData persistence instead of registry, no script execution from Markdown, denied new windows/navigation, explicit saves only, and custom CSS residual risks.
Security review completed. Source review found document writes limited to explicit Save/Ctrl+S, settings writes limited to AppData JSON, no registry APIs, no shell command execution, no Web storage persistence, no fetch/XHR/WebSocket calls, denied new WebView windows, and rendered Markdown sanitized with ammonia before innerHTML assignment. Tightened CSP to block network connections, remote images, frames, object/embed content, and base URI changes. Custom CSS is size-limited and filtered on both JS and Rust sides for @import, remote URLs, javascript:, expression(), behavior:, and -moz-binding. Dependency review noted ammonia locked to patched 4.1.2 for RUSTSEC-2025-0071 and rand transitive dependency locked to patched 0.8.6 for RUSTSEC-2026-0097. cargo-audit was not installed, so the advisory check used RustSec web records plus local Cargo.lock inspection.
Committed implementation and security review hardening in ec7a8999b447aee202f29b21dc3790f81c724cd1 (Persist theme settings and package icon).
close_reason: Security review completed, practical hardening applied, and residual dependency-audit limitation recorded.

title: Persist theme settings in AppData
description: Theme selection and custom CSS should persist across documents using a low-risk user-local settings file under AppData rather than per-document WebView localStorage or registry keys.
acceptance_criteria: App reads/writes settings under user AppData; selected built-in theme persists across documents; custom CSS persists across documents; no registry writes are used; validation passes.
notes:
Implementation started. Moving theme persistence out of WebView localStorage and into an AppData-backed settings JSON file handled by Rust IPC.
Implemented AppData-backed theme persistence in Rust IPC. Settings load from %APPDATA%\Markdown Reader\settings.json, fall back to LOCALAPPDATA/USERPROFILE AppData/temp when needed, and save normalized JSON. Removed WebView localStorage theme persistence. Added tests covering settings injection, unknown theme fallback, custom-theme validation, and custom CSS sanitization. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo build --release.
Committed implementation in ec7a8999b447aee202f29b21dc3790f81c724cd1 (Persist theme settings and package icon).
close_reason: Theme settings now persist across documents via AppData JSON with no registry writes, and validation passed.

title: Require explicit save for document writes
description: The app currently writes document changes during close/autosave behavior. Users should have to explicitly save with the save button or Ctrl+S; generated [toc] and rendered edits must remain in-memory until manual save.
acceptance_criteria: Closing the app does not save unsaved changes automatically; dirty documents prompt before close where practical; only save button/Ctrl+S write the file; validation passes.
notes:
Removed close-time autosave IPC; close now asks JavaScript to confirm dirty state and only exits. File writes remain limited to save button/Ctrl+S. [toc] expansion marks the document dirty in memory when generated.
Removed close-time autosave. Close now asks JS to confirm dirty state and only sends a close event; file writes are limited to save button/Ctrl+S. [toc] expansion updates editor state in memory and marks dirty when it changes content.
Additional review fix: removed stale close_pending guard so canceling the unsaved-changes close prompt leaves the app usable and future close attempts still work.
close_reason: Autosave-on-close removed; explicit save required and validated.

title: Remove Windows console window for release app
description: Configure the Rust Windows application so release builds launch without a black console window in the background while preserving useful console output in debug builds.
acceptance_criteria: Release Windows builds use the windows GUI subsystem; README documents debug vs release launch behavior; validation passes.
notes:
Added Windows GUI subsystem attribute for release builds and README note explaining debug terminal vs release GUI executable behavior.
Added cfg_attr(all(windows, not(debug_assertions)), windows_subsystem = windows) so release builds are GUI subsystem apps. Verified target\release\markdown-reader.exe PE subsystem = 2 (Windows GUI). README documents debug terminal vs release GUI behavior.
close_reason: Release executable configured and verified as Windows GUI subsystem.

title: Consolidate editor toolbar into main nav
description: Move formatted Markdown editing controls into the same nav row as save, toggle, and settings instead of a separate toolbar row.
acceptance_criteria: There is one nav row; formatted editing controls appear in that nav when useful; no separate toolbar row consumes vertical space; validation passes.
notes:
Moved formatted editing controls into the existing main nav row; removed separate toolbar row.
Implemented one-row navigation: formatted edit controls now live inside the same nav as Save, toggle, and Settings. The separate toolbar row was removed.
close_reason: Formatting controls consolidated into main nav and validated.

title: Fix plaintext editor vertical layout
description: Plaintext Markdown mode is only showing the scrollable text area in the top inch below the nav, leaving unused window space. Restore full-height editor layout.
acceptance_criteria: Plaintext textarea fills the available application area below the single nav; formatted view still fills the same area; validation passes.
notes:
Restored two-row body grid (nav + main) so plaintext textarea and rendered view fill the available window height.
Fixed plaintext layout by reverting body grid to two rows: 34px nav plus minmax(0, 1fr) main content. Removing the separate toolbar row lets plaintext textarea and rendered view fill the available window.
close_reason: Plaintext editor layout restored to full available height and validated.

title: Repair over-escaped about.md test file
description: Repair the user-reported Markdown file that was over-escaped by the prior rendered-view save bug: Y:\Shared\01. Relationships\03. Close Friends\Berg, Star\about.md. Create a backup before editing and only remove escape slashes matching the known bad serializer pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Backup exists; over-escaped Markdown characters are repaired; file remains readable Markdown; validation summary recorded.
notes: Repaired user file Y:\Shared\01. Relationships\03. Close Friends\Berg, Star\about.md. Created backup Y:\Shared\01. Relationships\03. Close Friends\Berg, Star\about.md.before-mdreader-unescape-20260528-215203.bak. Removed 346 bad bracket escapes from the prior rendered-save bug: 173 instances of \[ and 173 instances of \]. Verified no remaining matches for the old serializer escape pattern \\[`*_{}\[\]<>].
close_reason: User-reported damaged Markdown file repaired with backup and verification.

title: Fix formatted save Markdown fidelity
description: Formatted-view saves currently over-escape Markdown text, writing many backslashes after editing in rendered mode. Tighten HTML-to-Markdown serialization so saved documents remain valid, readable Markdown.
acceptance_criteria: Rendered-mode edits no longer over-escape ordinary punctuation/Markdown text; common structures serialize to valid Markdown; regression tests cover the escaping behavior.
notes:
Fix in progress. Removed blanket inline escaping in formatted-view serializer; added paragraph-start protection only for cases that would otherwise become Markdown block syntax. Added regression test checking the old global escape pattern is gone.
Fixed formatted-save fidelity by removing blanket escaping of inline text in the rendered-DOM-to-Markdown serializer. It now only protects paragraph-leading tokens that would accidentally become Markdown block syntax. Added a regression test to ensure the old global escape pattern is not present.
close_reason: Formatted-view Markdown serialization no longer over-escapes ordinary inline text; regression coverage added and validation passed.

title: Validate Markdown fidelity enhancements
description: Run formatting, compile, tests, clippy, build, and smoke checks after save-fidelity, formatting-toolbar, and TOC changes.
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo build, and relevant smoke checks pass or blockers are recorded.
notes: Validation passed after fidelity/toolbar/TOC changes: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test (7 tests), cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo build, CLI smoke checks, and bounded GUI startup smoke with [toc].
close_reason: Validation passed: fmt, check, tests, clippy, build, CLI smoke, and bounded GUI startup smoke.

title: Implement [toc] Markdown expansion
description: Support a [toc] marker in plaintext Markdown. When rendering, convert it to an actual table of contents based on document headings. When switching back to plaintext or saving rendered edits, persist a populated Markdown TOC.
acceptance_criteria: [toc] renders as a formatted table of contents; generated TOC links target actual heading anchors; switching back to plaintext produces a populated Markdown TOC; tests cover TOC generation.
notes:
Implementation in progress. Added Rust-side [toc] expansion based on parsed document headings, generated Markdown links with readable heading slugs, and returned expanded Markdown to the editor when rendering.
Implemented [toc] expansion. Rust scans Markdown headings outside fenced code blocks, generates unique readable heading slugs, replaces [toc] lines with nested Markdown links, renders those links to the same heading ids, and returns expanded Markdown to the editor when switching to formatted view.
close_reason: [toc] marker expansion, generated Markdown TOC links, heading slug ids, and rendered internal TOC navigation implemented and validated.

title: Add rendered-mode Markdown formatting controls
description: Add UI mechanisms in formatted view for applying Markdown-like structures such as heading levels, bullets, numbered lists, blockquotes, code, bold, italic, and links.
acceptance_criteria: Rendered view has compact formatting controls; controls apply expected DOM formatting; save/toggle serializes controls back to Markdown; validation passes.
notes:
Implementation in progress. Added compact formatted-view toolbar for block formats, bold, italic, inline code, links, bullet lists, numbered lists, blockquotes, and code blocks.
Added compact formatted-view toolbar: block style dropdown for paragraph/H1-H4/quote/code, buttons for bold, italic, inline code, links, bullet lists, and numbered lists. Toolbar is hidden in plaintext mode and serializes back through the existing Markdown save path.
close_reason: Rendered-mode formatting toolbar implemented and validated.

title: Validate enhanced Markdown Reader workflow
description: Run Rust formatting, compile, tests, clippy, and smoke checks after save, formatted editing, and theme settings changes.
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo build, and relevant CLI/GUI smoke checks pass or blockers are recorded.
notes: Validation started and passed locally after enhancement implementation: fmt, check, tests, clippy, build, CLI smoke, and bounded GUI startup smoke.
close_reason: Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo build, CLI smoke, and bounded GUI startup smoke.

title: Add save control and direct formatted editing
description: Add an explicit save button to the nav, keep Ctrl+S save behavior, and make the formatted Markdown view directly editable for practical small text changes.
acceptance_criteria: Nav has a save button; Ctrl+S and save button both persist the file; formatted view can be edited directly; switching back to plaintext reflects formatted-view edits in Markdown or a documented fallback; tests/checks pass.
notes:
Implementation in progress. Added save button UI, kept Ctrl+S path, made rendered article contenteditable, and added rendered-DOM-to-Markdown conversion for save/toggle back to plaintext.
Implemented save workflow and formatted-view editing. The nav now includes a save icon button, Ctrl+S uses the same save path, rendered Markdown is contenteditable, and rendered DOM edits are converted back to conservative Markdown before save or switching to plaintext. Conversion preserves common structures while normalizing source formatting where necessary.
close_reason: Save button, Ctrl+S save path, contenteditable rendered view, and rendered-DOM-to-Markdown sync implemented and validated.

title: Add theme settings modal
description: Add a single gear button that opens a settings modal. The modal should support premade CSS themes and a custom CSS file import/use path suitable for this WebView frontend.
acceptance_criteria: Nav has one gear button; modal opens/closes accessibly enough for keyboard/mouse use; user can select among premade CSS themes; user can import a custom CSS file and apply it; choice persists locally where practical; tests/checks pass.
notes:
Implementation in progress. Added gear button, settings modal, embedded CSS theme definitions, local theme application, and custom CSS file import via WebView File API/localStorage.
Implemented gear/settings workflow. The nav now includes one gear icon button; settings opens a modal with built-in CSS themes loaded from themes/*.css plus a custom CSS file input. Theme selection and custom CSS are persisted through localStorage when available.
close_reason: Gear settings modal, premade CSS themes, and custom CSS import/apply flow implemented and validated.

title: Improve Markdown editing workflow
description: Add explicit save controls, richer formatted-view editing, and user theme settings to the Rust Markdown Reader.
acceptance_criteria: UI has a save button and Ctrl+S save path; formatted Markdown view supports practical direct editing for small changes; nav includes one gear button that opens a settings modal; settings allow choosing premade CSS themes and importing/using custom CSS; behavior is validated with Rust checks and smoke tests.
notes: Enhancement pass completed. Added explicit save button, retained Ctrl+S, added direct formatted-view editing for common Markdown structures, added gear settings modal, added built-in CSS theme files, and added custom CSS import with local persistence where WebView storage permits.
close_reason: Enhanced editing and theme workflow implemented and validated locally.

title: Implement Rust Markdown reader MVP
description: Scaffold and implement the Rust desktop application with file argument loading, editable plaintext mode, formatted Markdown mode, minimal toggle navigation, save behavior, and section-aware scroll preservation.
acceptance_criteria: Cargo project exists; app opens provided Markdown path; content can be edited in plaintext; Ctrl+S or close persists changes; formatted view updates after edits; section-aware toggle behavior keeps the corresponding document area visible.
notes:
Implementation delegated to a worker after audit prompt-run record creation. Chosen starting approach: Wry/WebView2 shell, pulldown-cmark Markdown rendering, ammonia sanitization, DOM-based textarea/render scroll preservation by Markdown section.
Implementation worker outcome: added initial Rust/Wry Markdown reader MVP in Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, src/main.rs, and README.md. Behavior implemented: exactly-one path argument validation, large resizable Wry/WebView2 window, sparse nav toggle, rendered-default Markdown view, plaintext editing, Ctrl+S save, close autosave, Rust pulldown-cmark rendering with ammonia sanitization, and heading-index based scroll restoration between textarea and rendered view. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo build, argument smoke checks for zero/two/missing file args, and bounded GUI startup smoke with a temp Markdown file. Status: complete from worker perspective; ready for orchestrator review.
User clarified the app should include a normal Windows title bar for resize/maximize/close. Existing Wry default likely provided decorations, but implementation now explicitly sets with_decorations(true) on the WindowBuilder.
Orchestrator review accepted the implementation with one direct clarification patch: WindowBuilder now explicitly uses with_decorations(true), satisfying the normal Windows title bar requirement for resize/maximize/close. cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo build, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, CLI error-path smoke checks, and bounded GUI startup smoke all passed.
close_reason: Rust Markdown reader/editor MVP implemented and orchestrator-reviewed. Includes explicit normal title bar decorations, file argument loading, sparse toggle nav, plaintext editing, rendered Markdown view, save behavior, safe rendering, and heading-based location preservation.

title: Validate Markdown reader build and behavior
description: Run formatting, tests where available, cargo check/build, and a lightweight smoke validation for the Markdown reader. Record any limitations or follow-up work.
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt/check/build outcomes recorded; smoke validation for argument handling and source structure recorded; unresolved manual UI risks captured in Beads if any.
notes:
Validation started by orchestrator after implementation review.
Validation passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check; cargo test (2 tests passed); cargo build; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; CLI smoke for zero args, two args, and missing file all returned exit 1 with expected usage/read errors; bounded GUI startup smoke with temp Markdown file stayed alive for 5 seconds and was terminated.
close_reason: Validation suite passed: fmt, check, tests, build, clippy, argument smoke, and bounded GUI startup smoke.

title: Markdown file viewer/editor desktop app
description: Build a small Rust-based Windows desktop application that opens a positional Markdown file argument, shows a large resizable window, provides a minimal nav bar with a side-to-side toggle, switches between plaintext editing and formatted Markdown viewing, and preserves the current document location while toggling modes.
acceptance_criteria: Rust project builds; app accepts one positional .md path; default window is large and resizable but not fullscreen; nav bar is minimal and contains the view/edit toggle; plaintext mode supports editing and saving; formatted mode renders Markdown; toggling preserves the visible section/location; validation commands pass.
notes:
Originating user requirement is user-owned product direction from 2026-05-28.
MVP completed. Clarified title bar requirement is satisfied explicitly with decorated, resizable Wry window. File-association installer/registry setup remains out of scope for this first build; executable can be associated manually after build if desired.
close_reason: MVP implemented, reviewed, and validated locally.

title: Generate and package app icon
description: Generate a clean non-black-and-white icon for the Markdown Reader app, using an M and a differently colored down arrow or similar visual. Package it as a Windows icon if practical.
acceptance_criteria: Project contains generated icon asset; release Windows executable uses the icon where toolchain support is available; source PNG retained; validation passes or blocker recorded.
notes:
Generated icon draft using imagegen skill: dark teal M with contrasting orange down arrow on light rounded-square background. Packaging into project asset next.
Generated and packaged icon assets: assets/app-icon-source.png, assets/app-icon.png, and assets/app-icon.ico. Added build.rs with winresource to embed assets/app-icon.ico into Windows builds. Verified target\release\markdown-reader.exe remains Windows GUI subsystem and Windows can extract the associated icon. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo build --release.
Committed icon assets and Windows resource packaging in ec7a8999b447aee202f29b21dc3790f81c724cd1 (Persist theme settings and package icon).
close_reason: Generated icon assets are committed to the project and release executable embeds the icon.

title: Add Windows file-association helper
description: Optionally add an installer, registry helper, or documented command for associating .md files with the built markdown-reader.exe on Windows.
acceptance_criteria: User can install or run a helper so double-clicking .md files launches markdown-reader.exe with the file path as its single positional argument; rollback/uninstall instructions are documented.

title: Review GUI dependency choice
description: Compare lightweight Rust GUI approaches for this app and record the chosen approach with rationale and tradeoffs.
acceptance_criteria: Decision notes compare at least three viable approaches; chosen dependency set is justified for Windows Markdown viewer/editor use.
notes:
Decision review started. Comparing egui/eframe, Tauri, and Wry/WebView2 for a small Rust Windows Markdown reader/editor.
Dependency decision: compared (1) eframe/egui 0.34.3: pure Rust native GUI and no WebView, but rich Markdown rendering plus section-aware scroll sync would require more custom layout/control; (2) Tauri 2.11.2: mature desktop app framework using Wry, but adds project/build overhead and usually a frontend toolchain for a very small utility; (3) Wry 0.55.1 + WebView2 directly: Rust desktop shell with no Node build, built on the same webview layer Tauri uses, and the DOM makes textarea/rendered Markdown scroll mapping straightforward. Chosen approach: Wry direct shell with pulldown-cmark 0.13.x for Markdown conversion and ammonia 4.x for HTML sanitization. Tradeoff: Windows WebView2 runtime is expected; if absent, user may need the runtime installed.
close_reason: Chose direct Wry/WebView2 Rust shell after comparing egui/eframe, Tauri, and Wry. Decision recorded in Beads; implementation task will validate the dependency path.

title: Configure repository remote push destination
description: Repository has no Git remote configured, so git push cannot complete. Add a remote destination when the project should be backed up or shared.
acceptance_criteria: git remote -v shows a configured destination and git push succeeds; bd dolt push also has a configured remote if Beads remote sync is desired.
notes: Created during final handoff because git push failed: no configured push destination. bd dolt push skipped for the same reason. This is infrastructure-only and does not block the local Markdown Reader MVP.
